source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

navigation.confidence_reason: The current pRHL goal has local branch/suffix surgery signals, but the exact guard or statement order still needs inspection.
navigation.why_now: current pRHL state looks past the main opener: the useful next work is local branch/suffix surgery, not changing proof family.

preconditions: Use only when the visible goal has a concrete guard controlling the local branch.
After the case split, use `rcondt`/`rcondf` for forced branches, `swap` for statement order, indexed `wp` for suffixes, `conseq` for a smaller post, or `rnd{1}`/`rnd{2}` for one-sided samples.
If the guard fact needed for `rcondt`/`rcondf` is not in the precondition, treat that as an invariant repair signal.
Probe the smallest local block; do not fresh-restart just because this surgery is syntax-sensitive.
fast_track_probe.expected_next_shape: Branch-specific pRHL residuals where forced-condition, swap, indexed-wp, one-sided-random, or conseq steps can be checked.

anti_routes.reason: This is a branch-local/suffix alignment problem; a fresh restart throws away useful opener and invariant work.
One side has sampling or instrumentation before the suffix; use `rcond`, `swap`, or one-sided `rnd` before plain `wp`.
`sim` is usually too late/too strong before the guard and suffix shape are aligned.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect tactic_forms for swap, rcondt, rcondf, wp, conseq, rnd, or eager
inspect align or goal_info for statement indexes before retrying
use undo_to_checkpoint {} and choose the checkpoint before the call/loop invariant that should carry the missing guard or size fact
inspect lemma_hints or lookup_symbol for the local size/nth/bad-event lemma
downgrade this surgery route only after a smaller prefix and relevant tactic_forms fail

moves.title: Invariant-call route
moves.applicability: This is a `call (_: Inv)` route template, not a complete tactic; fill in the invariant expression from the current goal before probing or committing it.
moves.runnable_status: Not yet; replace `Inv` with a concrete EasyCrypt invariant before probing or committing this tactic.
moves.needs_frame: Postcondition shape only — no `={...}` equality frame. Build the `={...}` part from `application_context.write_map` (per candidate field: who writes it — the read-only/preserved fields are safe to equate), then add your coupling before this is a usable invariant.
how_to_complete: Read the current precondition, postcondition, and the two visible call sites; include the state that must be preserved across the adversary or oracle call.
If the call generates oracle obligations, include the global/oracle state needed by the named oracle-equivalence handles visible in the current context.

missing_input: Concrete EasyCrypt arguments for every placeholder in the displayed tactic.

frontier_alignment.summary: Frontier alignment: setup statements appear before the current frontier. Use this as a proof-reader map before choosing while/rnd/seq/call tactics.

rows.right: 3 setup statement(s): FinRO.init(); () <- x; D(A, G2(FinRO).CCRO).O.init()
rows.proof_read: These are setup statements before the current frontier; preserve the proof-relevant initialized state in later invariants, seq cuts, or calls.

procedure_navigation.how_to_use: Positions for choosing wp/sp depth, which `if` to rcond, and where the next loop/call frontier is. Facts only — pick the tactic and the branch direction yourself.

absorb_depth.sp_hint: sp 1 3 / wp -1 -3 absorbs the straight-line setup (1 left, 3 right) before the next frontier

checks: Read coverage and missing live facts before committing a seq cut.
A runnable seq candidate is not evidence that the invariant is strong enough.

evidence: distribution or lossless token remains visible
obligation_families.effect: The remaining pure goal may include invertibility or lossless side conditions from an earlier sampling alignment.
obligation_families.limitations: No distribution lemma is selected by this surface.

translation_facts: Decorations identify the program memory from which a term is read.
Pretty-printer memory names and introduced proof-context names can differ.
ambient_memory_translation.effect: Local claims in the pure tail depend on the currently introduced memory binders as well as displayed term decorations.

pure_tail_surface.effect: Summarizes verifier-visible pure-goal structure after program frontier actions have been discharged or hidden by local proof steps.
limitations: This panel records syntactic and proof-state facts from the current view.
It does not choose a tactic or instantiate a lemma.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
The current cut or frontier context may expose a call route.
ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.
ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations

ask_manager_for.use_when: The visible cut may depend on LHS/RHS statement alignment or missing live facts.
ask_manager_for.returns: swap/alignment context for the visible pRHL frontier

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid form for call, seq, while, rnd, or rewrite.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: The visible cut/frontier may need indexed `sp i j` before branch tactics.
Mid-proof pRHL suffix surgery may need indexed `wp`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix-alignment traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp`, `wp`, or `sim`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relation before `sim`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `conseq` forms and pRHL memory-annotation traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: One side may have an extra sample or need an explicit sample coupling.
A known statement-order mismatch may need an eager/lazy transformation.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `eager` forms and when to prefer smaller surgery
